rationale: The citation is used in the core theory section to support a key mechanism, not as a generic literature list item. The authors invoke Dhar et al. (2007) as prior evidence that consecutive actions can create a momentum-like mindset, which they use to justify their accessibility-to-immersion account for media choices. Although the 2022 paper does not reuse Dhar et al.'s experimental design or exact mechanism, it relies on that work as a conceptual precedent in building its framework.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the shopping momentum effect as a conceptual foundation that consecutive prior behavior can induce a momentum/mindset state. It leverages this precedent to frame why consecutive similar media consumption should increase category accessibility and immersion, leading to further similar consumption.
